content: We can reframe Self-Refine's generic iterative self-improvement pipeline as a multi-perspective reflection framework by integrating the inconsistent perspective principle from LLM self-reflection mechanisms: instead of having the same LLM repeatedly critique its own output, each iteration of Self-Refine draws on a distinct inconsistent solving perspective held by the same LLM to generate feedback. This turns the single-threaded refinement process of traditional Self-Refine into a 'self-multi-review' system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of needing no extra training or labeled data, while unlocking more critical feedback that avoids the blind spots of consistent single-perspective critique.
